🏃 Health and Physical Education

Dance and Acting

Express yourself through movement and drama!

📚 Unit 13 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 13: Dance and Acting

Dance and acting are performing arts that help you express emotions, tell stories, and connect with audiences.

Lesson 1: Dance

What is Dance?

Dance is movement of the body in a rhythmic way, usually to music. It is a form of expression, exercise, and entertainment.

Why Dance?

BenefitExplanation
Physical fitnessImproves flexibility, strength, endurance
CoordinationConnects brain and body
BalanceDevelops stability
ExpressionShow feelings without words
ConfidencePerforming builds self-esteem
Social skillsDancing with others teaches teamwork
Cultural connectionLearn traditional dances

Types of Dance

Classical Dances of India/Nepal

DanceOriginFeatures
BharatnatyamTamil Nadu (India)Fixed upper torso, bent legs, hand gestures (mudras)
KathakNorth IndiaSpins (chakkars), storytelling through footwork
ManipuriManipur (India)Graceful, rounded movements
Classical NepaliNepalTraditional court dances

Folk Dances of Nepal

DanceOrigin/CultureFeatures
MaruniMagar/GurungColorful dress, slow graceful moves
Dhan Nach (Rice dance)Limbu/KiratCelebrates rice harvest
Lakhey NachNewar (Kathmandu)Demon mask dance
DeudaFar-western NepalCircle dance, call and response
ChhokraTerai regionStick dance

Modern Dances (Age-appropriate)

DanceFeatures
Free styleAny movements you like
Hip-hop (basic)Rhythmic, energetic, street style
Creative movementExpress ideas through movement

Basic Dance Positions (Western Ballet Basics)

PositionFeetArms
First positionHeels together, toes outRounded in front of chest
Second positionFeet apart, toes outArms out to sides
Third positionOne foot in front of the otherOne arm up, one out
Fourth positionFeet apart, one in frontArms up and out
Fifth positionFeet crossed tightlyArms rounded above head

Basic Dance Elements (Time, Space, Energy)

ElementWhat it meansQuestions
TimeWhen movements happenFast/slow, beat/rhythm
SpaceWhere movements happenHigh/low, near/far, straight/curved
EnergyHow movements happenSharp/smooth, strong/light

Steps to Learn a Dance

StepAction
1Listen to the music several times
2Learn the basic beat (count 1-2-3-4, 1-2-3-4)
3Learn the footwork pattern
4Add arm movements
5Put together short sections
6Practice slowly, then faster
7Perform with expression (smile, energy)

Dance Etiquette

RuleWhy
Respect personal spaceDo not bump others
Stay in your spotDuring group dances
Listen to the teacherLearn correct steps
Help others who struggleBe kind and patient
Wear appropriate clothesComfortable, easy to move in
Warm up before dancingPrevents injury

Lesson 2: Acting

What is Acting?

Acting is pretending to be someone else or in a different situation to tell a story. Actors use their voice, body, and emotions to create characters.

Why Act?

BenefitExplanation
Self-confidencePerform in front of others
EmpathyUnderstand how others feel
CommunicationExpress clearly
CreativityUse imagination
Public speakingSpeak loudly and clearly
MemoryLearn lines and blocking

Basic Acting Skills

1. Voice

SkillHow to practice
ProjectionSpeak so the back row can hear
ClaritySay every word clearly
ExpressionChange pitch and tone for emotion
PaceSpeak faster for excitement, slower for sadness

2. Body

SkillHow to practice
PostureStand like your character (old person vs. child)
GesturesUse hands to show meaning
Facial expressionShow emotion on your face
MovementWalk the way your character walks

3. Emotions

EmotionHow to show it
HappySmile, bouncy walk, bright eyes
SadDownward mouth, slow movements, looking down
AngryTense body, fast movements, furrowed brows
ScaredWide eyes, frozen or shaking, step back
SurprisedEyes and mouth open, freeze

Acting Exercises

1. Mirror Exercise

StepAction
1Face a partner
2One person leads, one copies exactly (like a mirror)
3Move slowly – eyes, face, hands, body
4Switch leader

Purpose: Concentration, observation, connection

2. Emotion Walk

StepAction
1Walk around the room normally
2Teacher calls out an emotion
3Walk using that emotion (happy walk, sad walk, angry walk)
4Freeze when teacher says “stop”

Purpose: Body awareness, expression

3. Sound and Movement

StepAction
1Stand in a circle
2One person makes a sound and movement
3Everyone copies exactly
4Next person invents a new sound/movement

Purpose: Creativity, listening, group energy

4. Yes, Let’s! (Improvisation Game)

StepAction
1One person says “Let’s (do something)“
2Group responds “Yes, let’s!“
3Everyone does that action together

Example: “Let’s row a boat!” “Yes, let’s!” (everyone rows)

Purpose: Teamwork, imagination, saying yes to ideas

Simple Acting Scenarios for Class 5

ScenarioCharacterAction
Going to schoolStudentPut on bag, walk, say goodbye, meet friend
Eating a sour lemonAnyoneSmell, taste, sour face, reaction
Opening a birthday giftChildShake gift, open slowly, show excitement
Being very coldAnyoneShiver, rub arms, blow on hands, hurry
Being very hotAnyoneFan face, wipe sweat, look for shade

Elements of a Play/Drama

ElementDefinition
CharactersThe people in the story
SettingWhere and when the story takes place
PlotWhat happens (beginning, middle, end)
DialogueWords the characters speak
ConflictThe problem to solve
ResolutionHow the problem is solved

Preparing a Short Skit (5-10 minutes)

StepAction
1Choose a simple story (fable, folk tale)
2Decide the characters
3Write the script (what each person says)
4Assign roles to actors
5Practice lines at home
6Rehearse together (movement, timing)
7Add simple costumes and props
8Perform for the class or school

Example: Simple Skit Outline

Title: The Helpful Friend

SceneWhat happensCharacters
1Maya falls and drops her booksMaya, Rohan
2Rohan helps pick up booksMaya, Rohan
3They become friendsMaya, Rohan, Teacher

Stage Directions

DirectionMeaning
Stage leftActor’s left side (facing audience)
Stage rightActor’s right side
UpstageAway from audience (back of stage)
DownstageToward audience (front of stage)
Center stageMiddle of the stage
CrossWalk to another position
ExitLeave the stage
EnterCome onto the stage

Performance Tips

TipWhy
Face the audienceSo they can see you
Speak loudly and clearlySo everyone can hear
Do not turn your backKeeps you visible
Keep acting even if you forget a lineStay in character
Help your partner if they forgetWhisper the line
Stay in character until the endDo not break before curtain call
Have fun!Audience enjoys when you enjoy

Taking Care of Your Body as a Performer

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Warm up before dance/actingSkip warm-up
Drink waterStrain your voice
Rest when tiredPush through pain
Use safe movementsJump from high places
Respect other performersPush or shove on stage

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Dance is rhythmic movement of the ______.
  2. A traditional stick dance from the Terai region is called ______.
  3. In acting, the words the characters speak are called ______.
  4. Facing the audience is called ______.
  5. Moving toward the audience is called moving ______.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. Dancing improves physical fitness. (T/F)
  2. In acting, you should speak quietly so only your partner hears. (T/F)
  3. Lakhey Nach is a folk dance of Nepal. (T/F)
  4. The problem in a story is called the resolution. (T/F)
  5. Warming up before dancing prevents injury. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the Emotion to the Action

EmotionHow to show it
Happya) Tense body, fast movements
Sadb) Smile, bouncy walk
Angryc) Wide eyes, step back
Scaredd) Downward mouth, slow movements

Exercise 4: Name the Stage Direction

  1. Front of the stage (toward audience) → ______
  2. Back of the stage (away from audience) → ______
  3. Actor’s left side → ______
  4. Middle of the stage → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. body, 2. Chhokra, 3. dialogue, 4. facing the audience, 5. downstage

Exercise 2: 1. T, 2. F, 3. T, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Happy→b, Sad→d, Angry→a, Scared→c

Exercise 4: 1. Downstage, 2. Upstage, 3. Stage left, 4. Center stage

Great work! Dance and acting let you tell stories without words. Express yourself, and let your inner star shine!

🎯
